title: Named Entity Recognition Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of NER, BIO tagging, spaCy, and fine-tuning transformer models for entity extraction.

questions.question: In the BIO tagging scheme, what does the 'I-ORG' tag represent?

options.text: The beginning of an Organization entity
A token that is inside (continuing) a multi-token Organization entity
A token that is outside all named entities, belonging to an Organization context
An invalid or uncertain entity prediction

questions.question: What is the GPE entity type in spaCy's NER system?

options.text: General Purpose Entity — a catch-all for unclassified entities
Geopolitical Entity — countries, cities, states, and other politically defined locations
Grammatical Phrase Entity — noun phrases extracted by the parser
Group of People Entity — collective human groups

questions.question: In a NER training annotation, what does the tuple (23, 32, 'DRUG_NAME') represent?

options.text: Line 23, column 32, with entity label DRUG_NAME
The entity spans character positions 23 to 32 in the text and has the label DRUG_NAME
Token index 23, with 32 tokens in total, labeled DRUG_NAME
Sentence 23, paragraph 32 in the document corpus

questions.question: Why is F1-score per entity type a more useful evaluation metric than overall accuracy for NER?

options.text: F1-score is always more accurate than accuracy for any classification task
Overall accuracy is dominated by the 'O' (outside) tag which is very common, hiding poor performance on rare but important entity types
F1-score accounts for entity type confidence, while accuracy does not
NER models cannot be evaluated with accuracy because all predictions are multi-label

questions.question: What is the minimum recommended number of annotated examples per entity type when fine-tuning a NER model?

options.text: 10–20 examples — transformer models are few-shot learners
50–100 examples should be sufficient for all tasks
500–1000 examples per entity type for reliable generalization
At least 1 million examples — NER requires massive labeled datasets
